en.extract:
The knob-billed fruit dove (Ptilinopus insolitus) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is endemic to the Bismarck Archipelago.


Taxonomy and systematics
The knob-billed fruit dove was originally described as Ptilopus insolitus by Hermann Schlegel in 1863 on the basis of specimens from New Ireland. The name of the genus was later emended to Ptilinopus. The generic name Ptilinopus is derived from the Ancient Greek words πτιλον (ptilon), meaning feather, and πους (pous), meaning foot. The specific name insolitus is from the Latin word insolitus, meaning strange. Knob-billed fruit dove is the official common name designated by the International Ornithologists' Union. Other common names for the species include red-knobbed fruit dove and Schlegel's fruit-dove.

The knob-billed fruit dove is closely related to the orange-bellied fruit dove, and these two species are thought to form a species group with the grey-headed and carunculated fruit doves. This group may also include the black-naped and dwarf fruit doves.
There are two recognised subspecies of the knob-billed fruit dove. Variation between subspecies may be clinal.

Ptilinopus insolitus insolitus – Schlegel, 1863: The nominate subspecies. Found in the Bismarck Archipelago, except where inferior occurs.
Ptilinopus insolitus inferior – Hartert, 1924: Found on Mussau and Emirau. It is much smaller than the nominate subspecies and has russet feathering around the grey shoulder patch, and may represent a separate species.

The '''knob-billed fruit dove''' ('''''Ptilinopus insolitus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to the [[Bismarck Archipelago]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The knob-billed fruit dove was originally described as ''Ptilopus insolitus'' by [[Hermann Schlegel]] in 1863 on the basis of specimens from [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]].<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Bleeker|first1=P.|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/item/45346|title=Nederlandsch Tijdschrift voor de Dierkunde.|last2=Schlegel|first2=H.|last3=Westerman|first3=Gerardus Frederik|last4=Kerbert|first4=C.|last5=Koninklijk Zoologisch Genootschap Natura Artis Magistra|first5=te Amsterdam|date=1863|location=Amsterdam.}}</ref> The name of the genus was later [[Emendation (zoology)|emended]] to ''[[Ptilinopus]]''. The generic name ''Ptilinopus'' is derived from the [[Ancient Greek]] words πτιλον (''ptilon''), meaning feather, and πους (''pous''), meaning foot. The [[Specific name (zoology)|specific name]] ''insolitus'' is from the [[Latin]] word ''insolitus'', meaning strange.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Jobling|first=James A.|title=Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names|publisher=[[Christopher Helm]]|year=2010|isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4|location=London|pages=205, 322|language=en}}</ref> Knob-billed fruit dove is the official [[common name]] designated by the [[International Ornithologists' Union]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Pigeons – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/pigeons/|access-date=2021-11-11|language=en-US}}</ref> Other common names for the species include red-knobbed fruit dove and Schlegel's fruit-dove.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ptilinopus insolitus (Knob-billed Fruit-Dove) - Avibase|url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=77EDD972F7ACFEED&sec=summary|access-date=2021-11-19|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org}}</ref>
[[File:Ptilinopus insolitus - Abby Darrah - 582202637.jpeg|left|thumb|On tree branch]]
The knob-billed fruit dove is closely related to the [[orange-bellied fruit dove]], and these two species are thought to form a [[species group]] with the [[Grey-headed fruit dove|grey-headed]] and [[Carunculated fruit dove|carunculated fruit doves]]. This group may also include the [[Black-naped fruit dove|black-naped]] and [[Dwarf fruit dove|dwarf fruit doves]].<ref>{{Citation|last1=Baptista|first1=Luis F.|title=Knob-billed Fruit-Dove (Ptilinopus insolitus)|date=2020-03-04|url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/kbfdov1/1.0/introduction|work=Birds of the World|editor-last=Billerman|editor-first=Shawn M.|publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology|language=en|doi=10.2173/bow.kbfdov1.01|access-date=2021-11-20|last2=Trail|first2=Pepper W.|last3=Horblit|first3=H.M.|last4=Boesman|first4=Peter F. D.|last5=Garcia|first5=Ernest|s2cid=241198519 |editor2-last=Keeney|editor2-first=Brooke K.|editor3-last=Rodewald|editor3-first=Paul G.|editor4-last=Schulenberg|editor4-first=Thomas S.|url-access=subscription}}</ref>

There are two recognised subspecies of the knob-billed fruit dove.<ref name=":0" /> Variation between subspecies may be [[Cline (biology)|clinal]].<ref name=":1">{{Citation|last1=Baptista|first1=Luis F.|title=Knob-billed Fruit-Dove (Ptilinopus insolitus)|date=2020-03-04|url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/kbfdov1/1.0/introduction|work=Birds of the World|editor-last=Billerman|editor-first=Shawn M.|publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology|language=en|doi=10.2173/bow.kbfdov1.01|access-date=2021-11-20|last2=Trail|first2=Pepper W.|last3=Horblit|first3=H.M.|last4=Boesman|first4=Peter F. D.|last5=Garcia|first5=Ernest|s2cid=241198519 |editor2-last=Keeney|editor2-first=Brooke K.|editor3-last=Rodewald|editor3-first=Paul G.|editor4-last=Schulenberg|editor4-first=Thomas S.|url-access=subscription}}</ref>
* ''Ptilinopus insolitus insolitus'' – <small>[[Hermann Schlegel|Schlegel]], 1863</small>: The nominate subspecies. Found in the [[Bismarck Archipelago]], except where ''inferior'' occurs.<ref name=":1" />
* ''Ptilinopus insolitus inferior'' – <small>[[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1924</small>: Found on [[Mussau Island|Mussau]] and [[Emirau Island|Emirau]]. It is much smaller than the nominate subspecies and has russet feathering around the grey shoulder patch, and may represent a separate species.<ref name=":1" />
